title: Evangelion and summer Whats the connection
display_title: Evangelion and summer Whats the connection
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and summer. What's the connection?             Quote Post by BriarPatches » Mon Jul 04, 2011 2:00 am Why is summer so often affiliated with Eva? Whether it's the actual word, or summer aesthetics, I've come across it on Eva merchandise and in books many times. It's always just been a sort of implicit theme, but I've never found any real explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Jul 04, 2011 2:25 am It is always summer in Japan in the Eva world.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 04, 2011 3:08 am symbv wrote: It is always summer in Japan in the Eva world.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Jul 04, 2011 3:27 am ^ Very true. All the movies of Eva were shown during summer. Having said that, the TV series ran during fall and winter (1995 Oct to 1996 Mar) so at that time the connection to summer is mainly the season as seen in the anime.

[]: Re: Evangelion and summer. What's the connection?             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Jul 04, 2011 3:33 am BriarPatches wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jul 04, 2011 3:54 am I always thought the "summer" thing in Eva's universe was meant to be ironic, since humanity itself is more in the winter, or at least the fall. If you want to stretch, you could say it's the "summer" of Shinji's life, however. Sachi wrote: I've even heard the comparison that Eva to the Japanese is like Star Wars to Americans (which is relevant to why Anno is oft called the new George Lucas in terms of "ruining amazing franchises.").

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 04, 2011 4:41 am Warren Peace wrote: Oh who says that outside of the hysterics on this forum?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jul 04, 2011 4:53 am That madboards forum is essentially a clone of this one, so a rose by a different name. The other two links both reference George Lucas to point out how Hideaki Anno isn't really like him. They like Rebuild, and say the exact opposite of your initial characterization. They don't think he "ruined" anything.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Jul 04, 2011 5:12 am Warren Peace wrote: Oh who says that outside of the hysterics on this forum?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jul 04, 2011 5:26 am It is hysterics to compare Anno to George Lucas like that. Anno hasn't "ruined" Evangelion for the vast majority of Evangelion fans and Anime fans in general. Such a viewpoint resides in the extreme minority. I dismiss that "opinion" (such as it is) because it's too silly to take seriously. Anyway, that will be my last word on the topic because this thread is about something else.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 04, 2011 11:07 am Warren Peace wrote: That madboards forum is essentially a clone of this one, so a rose by a different name.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 04, 2011 11:10 am I can understand critics to Rebuild as its own work (actually it never happens) but saying that "he ruined Evangelion" is very childish; NGE is still NGE, it won't change because of his new vision of Evangelion, this is the new feeling of Evangelion that the director has, you can like it or dislike it, in the later case just go watching NGE+EoE for the nth time, but please stop bitching about Anno, anime are supposed to be a form of entertainment and NME is entertaining for many, many people. EDIT: Sachi wrote: When I visited Japan several years ago (before the NME buzz was being kicked about) it was hard to travel anywhere in Tokyo without seeing something Eva-related every couple blocks (not to mention Akihabara).

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Jul 04, 2011 12:30 pm I was just in Japan last year (September), and up on the Tokyo Metro Government observation deck there was a little souvenir shop that sold lots of umbrellas, the usual city postcards and such, and...Evangelion merchandise.

[]: Re: Evangelion and summer. What's the connection?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jul 04, 2011 4:30 pm EvangelionFan wrote: Oh look it's got Shinji on the cover- Wait, what? Why would they put Shinji on the cover? And he looks so... Shinji .

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Jul 04, 2011 4:53 pm I recall reading something from a Gainax staffer about how summer is thematically significant to Eva due to the fact that it is "the season in which a young man experiences growth", or something to that effect. Whatever the case may be, those droning cicadas make for some great atmosphere, I'll tell ya that right now. I'll never hear that sound inanother anime without thinking about existential despair. And yeah, I've never heard anyone outside of extreme fanboys - the kind who like to jump up, flail their arms about and declare franchise ruination at tge drop of a hat - proclaim Hideaki Anno tantamount to George Lucas. It's a vocal but well-in-the-minority perspective - far moreso than, say, the people who insist Evangelion is meaningless garbage that has ruined anime itself FOREVER . (Incidentally, I'm speaking as someone who neither particularly likes Rebuild nor particularly dislikes the Star Wars prequels.)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 5:46 pm BornIn1142 wrote: Is it very easy to dismiss the opinions of others by declaring them hysterics? I wonder whether I shouldn't adopt that tactic myself.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:47 pm gatotsu911 wrote: I recall reading something from a Gainax staffer about how summer is thematically significant to Eva due to the fact that it is "the season in which a young man experiences growth", or something to that effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 06, 2011 6:06 am The world of the endless summer ... is the season of incidents that lets a boy grow or the season that has a lot of homework in store.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Fri Jul 08, 2011 9:18 pm cyharding, the funny thing is that gatotsu was the one who typed up Refrain 's booklet for us! If anyone is interested in FreakyFilmFan4ever's claims, my standard reference for most of his assertions is The Secret History of Star Wars . As for me, cicadas don't bother me because of Eva... they bother me because of Higurashi .
summary:
[]: Evangelion and summer. What's the connection?             Quote Post by BriarPatches » Mon Jul 04, 2011 2:00 am Why is summer so often affiliated with Eva? Whether it's the actual word, or summer aesthetics, I've come across it on Eva merchandise and in books many times. It's always just been a sort of implicit theme, but I've never found any real explanation.

[]:           &
